I first came across the idea of ​​using Open Data 20 years ago and since then it has been my passion. In 2015, we launched Social Links company with my partners, for 3 years it was a hobby and search for PMF, since 2018 we have been actively growing and have become recognised world leaders in OSINT. We are testing how our products can empower other areas of the economy now, such as cybersecurity, finance, sales & marketing. There are successful pilots and projects already.

I will be glad to meet investors and partners who will help develop OSINT and Open Data market  together.

P4J2zrSZ7IXVISZDiPVL1ZuJ1aR2.bio:
Tricia Bitetto is an award-winning program manager, marketing strategist, and two-time startup founder renowned for orchestrating high-impact experiences that catalyze innovation and amplify brand narratives. As CIC expands to New York City in January 2026 as a key partner in the newly announced BATWorks climate innovation hub at the Brooklyn Army Terminal, Tricia’s work at the flagship Venture Café Cambridge puts her at the forefront of CIC’s mission to build innovation ecosystems that drive equitable economic opportunity and global collaboration.

As a driving force behind Venture Café Cambridge, the largest tech networking event in New England, Tricia has curated programming for over 300 speakers and welcomed more than 20,000 global visitors in 2024 alone. Currently in Boston, she leads with a blend of creative intuition and operational excellence, overseeing content strategy, speaker curation, marketing, and volunteer coordination.

Her collaborations with institutions like Harvard Business School, MIT, and Princeton University have positioned her to spearhead thought leadership programs and career development initiatives that bridge scholarly insight with entrepreneurial action. She has cultivated strategic partnerships with stakeholders across South America, Asia, Europe, and Africa—fostering international exchanges that connect startups, researchers, and ecosystem leaders through programming designed to accelerate cross-border collaboration and shared growth.

My approach transforms complex information into high-quality, trusted data assets that empower analytics, enable confident decision-making, and unlock scalable ML/AI capabilities. Outside of work, I’m a lifelong fan of sports-driven by a personal goal to experience and learn from as many disciplines as possible. Exploring new sports fuels my curiosity, discipline, and resilience, qualities I bring back into my professional life as well.

UjR2DgOKS4P4nk1hY2eXV8Cens72.name: Gavin Rowe
UjR2DgOKS4P4nk1hY2eXV8Cens72.bio:
I'm Gavin Rowe, a retired Chief Navy Diver with 16 years of service and CEO of Task Force VA.

My own transition out of the military is what led me to build Task Force VA. I didn't want anyone else to navigate what I had to, and the experience showed me the systemic challenges veterans face after they take off the uniform. The platform was designed to democratize VA disability claims for America's 18 million eligible veterans.
That lived experience now carries across several platforms we've built:

Task Force VA (taskforceva.ai) — veteran-built AI SaaS platform that gives Veterans and Active-Duty personnel instant access to rating criteria, claim preparation, and a vetted ecosystem of advocates, providers, and VSOs in one secure workflow.

Verdict Force  (Launching soon) — The legal product behind Task Force VA to support the B2B network containing the largest corpus of military-centric data to support the men and women who serve.

Recon Force (reconforce.org) —  a free, open-data nonprofit-sector intelligence and transparency platform that makes the financials of 2 million+ U.S. nonprofits searchable in seconds. 

NavyDeepSea (navydeepsea.com) — AI-powered iOS reference platform putting the entire U.S. Navy Diving Manual in every diver's pocket, with cited, manual-grounded answers built for safety-critical decisions.

Getting to work on these projects and put out technology for good has given me an enormous sense of purpose — it's something I look forward to every single day.

Exited founder + venture builder, helping startups and corporate teams go from zero to product-market fit—and beyond to exit. I thrive on turning ambitious ideas into real products and ventures with speed, clarity, and execution.

I’m the Founder of Build Momentum, a hands-on venture studio that makes it easier for founders, enterprise teams, and investors to spot high-impact opportunities, launch new ventures, and scale with confidence. We don’t just advise—we build alongside you. Through Fractional CPO leadership, 90-day innovation sprints, end-to-end product launches, and a network of seasoned venture operators, we reduce risk, save time, and help you unlock growth faster than going it alone.

I’ve led ventures across AI, enterprise SaaS, mobility, eCommerce, real estate, and mental health—taking companies from scratch to revenue, scaling into new countries and markets, leading cross-functional teams, and building corporate partnerships to unlock new growth. I operate globally, bilingual in English and Spanish, with experience building across borders.

Fun facts: I don’t shy from a challenge—once biking across the U.S. from Boston to San Francisco in 50 days. I’ve lived and worked in five countries (U.S., South Korea, the Philippines, Colombia, and Mexico). I also coach first-time LGBTQ founders. byos0GFY86SHMmBCIh0Z.name: Arizona Commerce Authority
byos0GFY86SHMmBCIh0Z.bio:
The Arizona Commerce Authority (ACA) is the state's leading economic development organization with a streamlined mission to grow and strengthen Arizona’s economy. The ACA uses a three-pronged approach to advance the overall economy: recruit, grow, create – recruit out-of-state companies to expand their operations in Arizona; work with existing companies to grow their business in Arizona and beyond; and partner with entrepreneurs and companies large and small to create new jobs and businesses in targeted industries.

The ACA is overseen by a prestigious public-private sector board of directors bringing together industry, academia and government. Chaired by Arizona Governor Katie Hobbs, the board is comprised of the state’s most prominent private-sector business leaders, university and community college presidents, and elected officials.

The ACA’s executive management team drives the organization’s day-to-day operations, ensuring it maintains focus on the recruitment of quality companies and jobs to the state of Arizona. This team brings together the best and brightest in economic development, management and communications.

Through educational workshops, financial assistance, mentorship programs, and community events, we aim to uplift and empower women to reach their fullest potential and make a positive impact in their communities. Join us as we work to create a more inclusive and equitable society where every woman has the opportunity to thrive and succeed.
